title: How to save culture in EU research and innovation funding
content:
The next Horizon Europe programme (2028–2034) is being shaped right now, and culture is disappearing from its architecture.

The European Commission’s proposal for the EU long-term budget has no dedicated component for culture or artistic research. The European Parliament’s draft report on Horizon doesn’t fix the proposal: culture gets a nod as a ‘horizontal principle,’ but receives no dedicated home similar to the current Cluster 2. That means less ring-fenced budget, less visibility, and fewer dedicated calls. The draft also proposes to dissolve Horizon’s Society pillar entirely, folding it into a Competitiveness pillar as a fifth policy window.

For this part of the Horizon programme, horizontal means everywhere in theory, nowhere in practice.

That’s why Michael Culture Association, together with the Artistic Research Alliance which is coordinated by ELIA and alongside

title: Webinar: Designing educational games combining sport and culture: digital escape games and geocaching
content:
How can we create fun and educational experiences centred on European sporting heritage?

Join us on April 28, at 3,30pm CEST for an exciting webinar organised as part of the My Sporting Heritage project (Erasmus+). This project brings us together with great partners committed to promoting Europe’s sporting heritage: the National Museum of Sport, the University of Amsterdam, Think Tank Sport et Citoyenneté, European Cultural Route of Sport and Radiona.

Registration:  https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeSbDeg9nu0CxfxYMaVo69GpgQXV9nxsZh3-t8N4B1T7GFr1w/viewform?usp=publish-editor

We will discuss and share resources on the theme of designing educational games that combine sport and culture, including digital escape games and geocaching! A glimpse of the programme:
1. Presentation of the My Sporting Heritage project
2. Presentation of the
